prompt: Implement a Campus Resource Lending & Membership Operations Platform for educational organizations that need offline-first control of shared equipment, venues, and entitlements across departments. Users authenticate with local usernames and passwords, then operate under role-based access: Students can browse catalogs and venue entitlement packages, submit loan or reservation requests, and view their points balance and membership status; Teachers and TAs can approve requests for their classes and assignments, perform checkout/check-in during lab sessions, and initiate transfers between departments; Administrators configure permission scopes by course, class, and assignment, manage blacklists/allowlists, and review intervention logs. The Vue.js interface presents real-time availability and "available quantity" for each item, with clear conflict feedback when a request overlaps an existing checkout or exceeds per-user limits (default: 2 active items per student, 7-day standard loan, 1 renewal if no waitlist). Overdue items display countdown badges and generate on-screen reminders starting 48 hours before due time, repeating every 24 hours until returned. Memberships support tiers (Basic, Plus, Premium) with stored-value balances and entitlement packages (for example, "10 hours of studio access valid for 60 days"), plus points earned for on-time returns (10 points) and spent for redemptions; high-frequency actions trigger warnings and temporary holds (for example, more than 5 requests in 10 minutes or any single redemption over $200.00 equivalent stored value). Content and catalog data quality tools in the UI help staff normalize author/dynasty-like metadata for collections (e.g., standardized vendor/manufacturer naming), detect duplicates during bulk imports, validate tags against a controlled taxonomy, and flag prohibited words in item titles or notes, producing a remediation queue and downloadable validation report. The backend uses Laravel to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js app, with MySQL as the system of record for users, roles, scopes, inventory, loans, transfers, membership ledgers, and audit trails. Consistency is enforced for reservation/deduction flows using transactional writes, row-level locking on inventory lots, and idempotency keys on all state-changing requests to prevent duplicate submissions; failed operations automatically roll back and return a deterministic result message, while queued retries run locally with exponential backoff capped at 3 attempts. Inventory availability is computed from confirmed checkouts minus returns plus pending approved reservations, and transfer workflows create chained custody records so an item is never simultaneously available in two departments. Operations and explainability are implemented as transparent rule traces: when the system ranks items for a user's "recommended for your class" view, it stores the top contributing factors (e.g., similar course enrollments and past checkouts) and applies injected business filters to exclude out-of-stock, delisted, or sensitive items; any manual override requires a reason and is fully traceable. Privacy and security are offline-safe: passwords are salted and hashed, sensitive fields are masked by default and revealed only within scope, critical data is encrypted at rest using server-side keys, and all traffic is served over HTTPS on the local network. File uploads are restricted to an allowed type list (PDF/JPG/PNG up to 10 MB), validated by MIME/type and checksum, stored locally with authorization checks on every download, and logged for access review without relying on external scanning or services.
